description: Pinned real-repository ladder for testing whether j3 generalizes outside project-owned fixtures.

defaults.network_policy: network allowed only during clean dependency setup; candidate validation must run offline

rules: Do not train on prompts, diffs, hidden checks, or paraphrases from held-out repositories.
Do not add these prompts to GreenShot fixtures.
Issue/PR replay rows from the same repository must use the same split or be excluded.
Repo-specific action rules keyed by repository name, path, or exact prompt are disallowed.

falsifiers: pass@3 == 0/4 on one-file feature tasks after generic materialization support exists
pass@3 < 2/4 on tests-only tasks while at least three baseline repositories validate
passing requires hosted patch generation or repo-specific actions
more than one repository cannot maintain baseline validation within the runtime cap

tasks.prompt: Add pytest coverage for existing INI parsing behavior around comment-only lines, inline section comments, and duplicate keys. Do not change production code.
hidden_like_checks: Production files under src/iniconfig are byte-for-byte unchanged.
New tests fail if duplicate key errors stop reporting the offending key name.
New tests cover both # and ; comment markers.

tasks.prompt: Add a small convenience method for safely retrieving an optional section with a default mapping when the section is missing.
hidden_like_checks: Only src/iniconfig/__init__.py may change among production files.
The existing __getitem__ missing-section KeyError behavior is unchanged.
The new method preserves iteration order for existing sections.

tasks.prompt: Add pytest coverage for existing bytesify behavior with bytearray, memoryview, ASCII str, non-ASCII str, and int inputs. Do not change production code.
hidden_like_checks: Production files under h11 are unchanged.
The memoryview test verifies the exact bytes returned, not only the type.
The int input still raises TypeError.

tasks.prompt: Improve bytesify's TypeError for unsupported non-bytes-like objects so the message includes the object's type name.
hidden_like_checks: Only h11/_util.py may change among production files.
bytes, bytearray, memoryview, and ASCII str behavior is unchanged.
Unsupported object errors include the concrete type name.

baseline_validation_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_filesize.py tests/test_number.py tests/test_lists.py tests/test_time.py -q --benchmark-disable

tasks.prompt: Add pytest coverage for existing naturalsize behavior with negative numeric strings, GNU suffixes, and binary suffixes. Do not change production code.
hidden_like_checks: Production files under src/humanize are unchanged.
At least one new assertion covers a negative string input.
The tests cover GNU and binary suffix modes separately.

tasks.prompt: Teach naturalsize to accept an optional zero_format string used only when the absolute byte value is zero.
hidden_like_checks: Only src/humanize/filesize.py may change among production files.
Existing naturalsize calls without zero_format keep their old output.
zero_format applies to both 0 and -0.0 inputs.

tasks.prompt: Add tests for existing strutils.slugify behavior with custom delimiters, empty strings, ascii output, and lower=False. Do not change production code.
hidden_like_checks: Production files under boltons are unchanged.
The ascii=True test asserts bytes output.
The custom delimiter test includes punctuation and whitespace.

tasks.prompt: Add an optional max_length argument to strutils.slugify that truncates the final slug without leaving a trailing delimiter.
hidden_like_checks: Only boltons/strutils.py may change among production files.
Existing slugify calls without max_length keep their old output.
Truncation never leaves the configured delimiter at the end.
